title: Evacuation of Kubindi
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Conjecture|legends}} {{Battle |prev=*[[Battle of Fondor]]''[[The New Jedi Order: Balance Point]]'' *Invasion of the [[Hutt Space/Legends|Hutt Space]] **[[Battle of Nal Hutta]] **[[Battle of Ylesia (26 ABY)|Battle of Ylesia]]''[[The New Jedi Order Sourcebook]]'' *Campaign on the [[Corellian Run/Legends|Corellian Run]] **[[Battle of Kalarba]]''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' **[[Battle of Druckenwell (Yuuzhan Vong War)|Battle of Druckenwell]] **[[Battle of Falleen (Yuuzhan Vong War)|Battle of Falleen]] *[[Battle of Kegan]]''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' |conc=*Conquest of the Hutt Space *[[Battle of Rodia (Yuuzhan Vong War)|Battle of Rodia]] |next=[[Battle of Duro (Yuuzhan Vong War)|Battle of Duro]] |image=[[File:KubindiYVW-TEA.jpg]] |name=Evacuation of Kubindi |conflict=[[Yuuzhan Vong War]] |date=[[26 ABY/Legends|26 ABY]] (c. 61:3)''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' places the start of [[The New Jedi Order: Balance Point|''Balance Point'']] ten months after the [[Destruction of Sernpidal]], which occurs in 60.5 per ''[[The Essential Atlas]]''. |place=[[Kubindi/Legends|Kubindi]] |result=Yuuzhan Vong victory |side1=*[[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] **[[Kubaz/Legends|Kubaz]] |side2=[[Yuuzhan Vong]] |side3= |side4= |commanders1=[[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Kyp Durron]] |commanders2=[[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Mujmai Iinan]] |commanders3= |commanders4= |unit1= |unit2= |unit3= |unit4= |forces1=[[Kyp's Dozen|Dozen-and-Two Avengers]] |forces2=[[Yorik-et|Coralskippers]] |forces3= |forces4= |casual1= |casual2= |casual3= |casual4= |civilian=Most of the population evacuated, while those left behind were [[Slavery/Legends|enslaved]] by the Yuuzhan Vong }} {{Quote|Noble news out of Kubindi, though. Tragic, but noble.|[[Randa Besadii Diori]], to [[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]]|}} During the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], in [[26 ABY/Legends|26 ABY]], the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kubindi/Legends|Kubindi]] was '''attacked''' by the [[Yuuzhan Vong]]. The invaders were drawn to Kubindi because of the [[Kubaz/Legends|Kubaz]] history of genetically engineered insects. However, most of the population evacuated before the attack, thanks to the efforts of the [[Kyp's Dozen|Dozen-and-Two Avengers]], a group led by the Jedi [[Kyp Durron]]. ==History== ===Invasion=== {{Quote|Our only communication has come courtesy of Jedi Kyp Durron. I’m sure you’ve seen that on HoloNet.''"
"''Who hasn’t? I suppose Jedi Corran Horn has returned to his usual heroics as well, by now.|[[Ayddar Nylykerka]] and [[Sien Sovv]]|}} [[File:Kubaz_NEGAS.jpg|thumb|left|180px|A [[Kubaz/Legends|Kubaz]], a native of Kubindi]] In [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]], the [[Yuuzhan Vong galaxy|extra-galactic]] race of invaders, the [[Yuuzhan Vong]], launched a massive [[Yuuzhan Vong War|conquest campaign]] directed against all worlds of the [[Galactic community]].''[[The New Jedi Order: Vector Prime]]'' [[26 ABY/Legends|Less than a year later]],{{GamerCite|8|I, Yuuzhan Vong}} after being [[Battle of Fondor|defeated]] at [[Fondor/Legends|Fondor]] the Yuuzhan Vong Commander [[Nas Choka]] retaliated by swiftly conquering all of [[Hutt Space/Legends|Hutt Space]].''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' The Yuuzhan Vong forces then attacked [[Attack on Junkfort Station|Junkfort Station]] and the planet [[Battle of Kegan|Kegan]], after which they proceeded directly toward the planet [[Kubindi/Legends|Kubindi]]. The choice of such a target for the attack was explained by the fact that Kubindi's history of [[Insecticulture|genetically engineering]] insect species had made the planet’s resources appealing to the Yuuzhan Vong. The peaceful, cultured [[Kubaz/Legends|Kubaz]] were no challenge to the Yuuzhan Vong; however, the [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Kyp Durron]], who was stationed at [[Bothawui/Legends|Bothawui]], had anticipated the attack and brought [[Kyp's Dozen|his own forces]] to the planet. Because the Yuuzhan Vong [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Mujmai Iinan]] had proposed that Kubindi could be taken with only half the usual number of [[Yorik-et|coralskippers]], Kyp's Dozen was able to single-handedly hold off''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' the reduced force long enough to allow all [[Starship/Legends|spaceworthy ships]] to flee offworld, and most of the population evacuated before the war fleet came within striking range. The displaced Kubaz had no choice but to join the inestimable ranks of [[Refugee/Legends|refugees]].''[[The New Essential Guide to Alien Species]]'' ===Aftermath=== ====Nearest influence==== {{Quote|Word is out that Kyp’s Dozen held off a Yuuzhan Vong attack force long enough that the Kubaz got every spaceworthy ship offplanet. You cannot call that anything less than heroic.|Randa Besadii Diori, to Jacen Solo|}} The actions of Kyp Durron and the Dozen-and-Two Avengers during the evacuation of Kubindi had a positive effect on their image and were covered on the [[HoloNet/Legends|HoloNet]]. However, [[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]] regarded Durron's heroics as inappropriate and willful. Serious consequences awaited the Yuuzhan Vong lieutenant Mujmai Iinan. After learning of the loss of so many potential sacrifices, the [[Warmaster]] [[Tsavong Lah]] had Iinan sacrificed to the [[Yun'o|the gods]] following the battle. Soon afterward, the [[New Republic Senate/Legends|Senator]] [[Viqi Shesh]], who sought to become the head of a new collaborationist government, contacted the Warmaster Tsavong Lah in an attempt to bring the war to a peaceful end. She described the simultaneous withdrawal from Kubindi and [[Battle of Rodia (Yuuzhan Vong War)|Rodia]] as a gesture of goodwill, but her proposal was ignored. Many of the worlds and star systems conquered at that time, including Kubindi, had been home to some of the most visible and active starfaring species. Whether by design on the part of the invaders or merely by coincidence, this strategy gave further fuel to the arguments of [[Speciesism|non-Human activists]]. The most paranoid believed that the New Republic leadership had somehow orchestrated a conspiracy to do what the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]] had failed to do – to kill all non-Humans. ====Further destiny of Kubindi==== When the Yuuzhan Vong took control of Kubindi, they [[Slavery/Legends|enslaved]] those left behind on the planet and forced them to assist in altering Kubindi's environment to grow food for the invaders. Later, the invaders exterminated nearly every [[Insect/Legends|insect]] species that had been cultivated by the Kubaz. This left the surviving population with little or no food, which, if left unchecked, would have wiped out every Kubaz on the planet within a [[Standard year/Legends|year]] – if not within [[Standard month/Legends|months]]. Kubindi was not liberated before the end of the war, when the [[Sekot accords|surrender]] of the invaders was signed.''[[The New Jedi Order: The Unifying Force]]'' Following the Yuuzhan Vong's defeat, the remaining Kubaz, greatly diminished in number, began the process of returning to their homeworld to rebuild their fractured society and to restore its former glory. ==Behind the scenes== The Battle of Kubindi was first mentioned in the sixth novel of the [[New Jedi Order]] series, ''[[The New Jedi Order: Balance Point]]'', which was published in [[2000]]. The event was briefly described, serving only as a tense backdrop for the narrative rather than receiving detailed coverage. Although the events of the novel took place less than a year after those of the series' first book, ''[[The New Jedi Order: Vector Prime]]'', which was set in [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]], the 2008 sourcebook ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' erroneously listed the date of the battle as [[27 ABY/Legends|27 ABY]]. ==Appearances== *''[[The New Jedi Order: Balance Point]]'' {{1stm}} *''[[The New Jedi Order: Force Heretic III: Reunion]]'' {{Imo}} ==Sources== *''[[The New Jedi Order Sourcebook]]'' *''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' *''[[The New Essential Guide to Alien Species]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' {{C|only the map}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{YuuzhanVongWarNav}} {{Interlang |es=Batalla de Kubindi }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kubindi, battle of}} [[Category:Battles of Kubindi]] [[Category:Battles of the Yuuzhan Vong War]]
